growth_records.strain: C5 (isolated from nitrate-contaminated groundwater, Oak Ridge National Laboratory, TN)
growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: grown with 8 mM lactate as carbon source and electron donor and 3 ml of sterile air injected ... we were able to calculate a specific growth rate of 0.4 ± 0.02 h−1 and a generation time of 1.73 ± 0.09 h (Figure 4B Inset)
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch on lactate+O2 minimal medium, 30C, 225 rpm, pH 7.2 (bicarbonate-buffered). Reported mu=0.4 +/- 0.02 h-1; doubling_time 1.73 +/- 0.09 h (reported); consistent with ln2/0.4=1.733 h. Medium minimal salts listed; nitrate not present in this O2-fed condition. No uptake/secretion rates measured. Full lactate/nitrate base medium recipe given in Methods (trace elements/vitamins per Yoon 2013 and Hillesland 2014, not itemized here).

growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: anaerobic versus microaerobic conditions, which were also found to have slightly different growth rates (Figure 5A; microaerobic μ = 0.2 h−1; anaerobic μ = 0.18 h−1)
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch, microaerobic lactate/nitrate minimal medium, 30C, pH 7.2, 225 rpm. Reported mu=0.2 h-1. doubling_time_h=3.47 CALCULATED = ln2/0.2 = 0.6931/0.2 = 3.466 h (not reported). No uptake/secretion rates. Lactate concentration not restated for this condition (8 mM used in the O2 condition); nitrate present as alternative electron acceptor.

growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: microaerobic μ = 0.2 h−1; anaerobic μ = 0.18 h−1
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch, anaerobic lactate/nitrate minimal medium, 30C, pH 7.2, 225 rpm. Reported mu=0.18 h-1. doubling_time_h=3.85 CALCULATED = ln2/0.18 = 0.6931/0.18 = 3.851 h (not reported). Nitrate-reducing (denitrification + respiratory ammonification). No uptake/secretion rates measured.

growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: The cultures growing in complex media (LB) under microaerobic conditions exhibited highly reproducible growth rates (μ = 0.24 ± 0.004 h−1)
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch on complex LB medium, microaerobic, 30C, 225 rpm. Reported mu=0.24 +/- 0.004 h-1. doubling_time_h=2.89 CALCULATED = ln2/0.24 = 0.6931/0.24 = 2.888 h (not reported). LB composition not itemized (named only). No uptake/secretion rates measured.

growth_records.medium: Nitrate-reducing minimal medium (ammonium as assimilatory N-source; aerobic)

up.method: ion chromatography (Dionex ICS-5000+); rate not reported

growth_records.cite: Vuono DC et al. Resource Concentration Modulates the Fate of Dissimilated Nitrogen in a Dual-Pathway Actinobacterium. Front Microbiol (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00003. [PMC6349771]
growth_records.method: reported (mu = 0.4 +/- 0.02 h-1); td calculated ln2/0.4 = 1.73 h
growth_records.snippet: when grown on 8 mM lactate, O2, and 1.5 mM ammonium as N-source, I. calvum displayed a typical growth curve with a specific growth rate of 0.4 +/- 0.02 mu (1.7 +/- 0.1 doublings/hour)
growth_records.notes: Aerobic reference condition. mu = 0.4 +/- 0.02 /h reported. Paper text states '1.7 +/- 0.1 doublings/hour' but this is inconsistent with mu=0.4 (which gives 0.577 doublings/h); ln2/0.4 = 1.73 h doubling time, so the '1.7' most likely refers to doubling TIME (h), not doublings/hour. No aerobic growth observed with nitrate as N-source. Mean pH across all vessels 7.3 +/- 0.05. Lactate uptake and product secretion rates not reported for this aerobic condition.

growth_records.medium: Nitrate-reducing minimal medium (8 mM lactate, 12 mM nitrate, ammonium-replete; C:NO3=2)

up.units: mM (8 mM supplied; oxidized predominantly completely to CO2)
mM (12 mM supplied, reduced to nitrite then N2O/NH4+)

sec.units: 100-200 uM (concentration range in media; minor incomplete lactate oxidation)

sec.method: gas chromatography; amount in Fig.3A/Supp Table S4
ion chromatography (cation, CS-16); amount in Fig.3A

sec.units: mM (major extracellular end-product; not fully consumed even at 300 h)

growth_records.cite: Vuono DC et al. Resource Concentration Modulates the Fate of Dissimilated Nitrogen in a Dual-Pathway Actinobacterium. Front Microbiol (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00003. [PMC6349771]
growth_records.method: reported qualitatively (growth curve in Fig.3A; growth coupled to nitrate reduction, slowed at ~50 h); numeric mu only in figure/Supp Table S5
growth_records.snippet: Some acetate was observed in the media (100-200 uM range), indicating minor incomplete lactate oxidation, however, I. calvum predominately oxidizes lactate completely to CO2. ... ammonium and N2O production began simultaneously, as soon as
growth_records.notes: Main anaerobic time-series condition (C:NO3 ratio = 2). Growth coupled to nitrate (not nitrite) reduction; growth slowed at ~50 h. Acetate accumulation 100-200 uM (experimental, concentration not rate). N2O and NH4+ amounts in Fig.3A / Supp Table S4 (not numerically in main text). Per-treatment growth rates (Fig.2C, ratios 4-0.1) only in figure/Supp Table S5, not extractable as numbers. NH4Cl 1.4 mM for ammonium-replete.

growth_records.medium: Nitrate-reducing minimal medium, ammonium-FREE (8 mM lactate, 12 mM nitrate; state-transition)

up.method: ion chromatography; not quantified as rate

up.units: consumed (no net NH4+ detected despite production; assimilated for growth)
up.method: ion chromatography; inferred from cell increase

growth_records.cite: Vuono DC et al. Resource Concentration Modulates the Fate of Dissimilated Nitrogen in a Dual-Pathway Actinobacterium. Front Microbiol (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00003. [PMC6349771]
growth_records.method: reported: cell counts +29% over inoculum (~1.8 generations) over 48 h in ammonium-free media
growth_records.snippet: cell counts increased by 29% over the inoculum in the ammonium-free media ... over a 48 h incubation ... Net ammonium production was 13 +/- 2.7 umoles with the remainder of dissimilated N being used by the denitrification pathway (24 +/- 4.
growth_records.notes: State-transition condition demonstrating functional NrfA and NH4+ re-assimilation. Secretion amounts are totals (umoles over 48 h), not per-gDW rates (biomass not given). Text also states 'a factor of 3.5x, or 1.8 generations with a net total of 5.4x10^5 +/- 8.9x10^4 cells/mL', which is internally ambiguous vs the '29% increase' (likely different reference points); growth rate not reliably derivable. If 1.8 generations over 48 h: mu = 1.8*ln2/48 = 0.026 /h (reported here only in notes given the ambiguity).

growth_records.medium: Nitrite-reducing minimal medium (8 mM lactate, 12 mM nitrite as sole acceptor; C:NO2=2)

up.units: mM (12 mM supplied as terminal acceptor)
up.method: ion chromatography; not quantified as rate

growth_records.cite: Vuono DC et al. Resource Concentration Modulates the Fate of Dissimilated Nitrogen in a Dual-Pathway Actinobacterium. Front Microbiol (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00003. [PMC6349771]
growth_records.method: reported qualitatively: no immediate growth observed with nitrite as sole acceptor
growth_records.snippet: When cultures were given nitrite, instead of nitrate as a terminal electron acceptor (8 mM lactate, 12 mM nitrite; ratio = 2), we observed no immediate growth (as was observed with nitrate) but measured more N2O than ammonium production (33
growth_records.notes: Nitrite-as-acceptor condition (Supp Fig S5). With nitrite denitrification (N2O) exceeds ammonification, opposite to low-C nitrate conditions. Secretion amounts are totals (umoles), not per-gDW rates. No immediate growth so no growth rate.
